summary: Recovered baseline contract + ranked CUDA optimization directions (Codex gpt-5.5:high task4 consult + Claude analysis).
directions: BF16 N=128 RMSNorm: 16 lanes/row, 2 rows/warp, FP32 accum, half-warp reduction, 4 warps/CTA, vectorized 128-bit load/store, grid-stride huge-M
FP32 N=5120 LayerNorm: 1 CTA/row, 256 threads, 5 float4/thread, registers-resident, mean then biased variance sum((x-mean)^2)/N, no --use_fast_math
rejected: constant-memory weight (full-row vector reads, not same-address)
fusion/CUDA-graph (changes callsite contract, out of scope)
reason: Directions ranked; proceeding to implement RMS (task5) and LN (task6).

summary: Native CUDA rms_norm_bf16_n128 + layer_norm_fp32 kernels behind strict exact-shape dispatcher; correct on all 6 captured shapes; geomean 1.695x over SGLang baseline on idle H200 (GPU7).

design.rms_bf16_n128: 16 lanes/row, 2 rows/warp, uint4 (8 bf16) vectorized load/store, FP32 accum, half-warp shfl_xor reduction, rsqrtf, 256 threads/CTA=16 rows/CTA, grid-stride cap 132*32
design.layer_norm_fp32: 1 CTA/row, 256 threads, 5 float4/thread, x retained in registers, mean then biased var sum((x-mean)^2)/N, rstd=1/sqrtf (precise), no --use_fast_math

correctness: PASS 33/33 (KDA_RUN_CORRECTNESS=1 pytest): 6 captured (CUDA path) + 5 adversarial fp32 + canonical regression grid (fallback==baseline) + one-pass cross-checks + dispatch-gate. RMS bf16 bitwise-identical to baseline; LN fp32 err 2.26e-6 (<1e-5).
lineage: kda_kernels rotary_embedding/_impls/b200/wrapper.py (JIT build + strict dispatch + recorder pattern)
reason: SUPERSEDED (do not cite): the round-0 fp32-fast LayerNorm failed the strict 1e-5 adversarial ceiling (fixed by the double-precision LN in s4-round1), and the 1.12x-2.58x speedups / idle_before_after=util0%mem124MB predate the validated clean idle-before+after benchmark (s6-round3-clean). Historical record only.

summary: NCU + roofline confirmation of the active bound for representative buckets (H200 GPU7).
reason: SUPERSEDED (do not cite): this round-0 NCU concluded LN was DRAM-bandwidth-bound at 78.23%, which no longer holds after the double-precision LN change. The current LN is mixed memory/compute (DRAM 62.7% / SM 56.7%); see s4-round1 + profile/ncu_round0/REPORT.md. RMS values still hold. Historical record only.

summary: Adversarial review (Codex gpt-5.5:high task9): NO P0 blockers. Confirmed LN smem reduction race-free, RMS half-warp shfl_xor reduction + 16B uint4 alignment valid for captured contiguous [M,128] bf16, no hidden per-shape regression.
findings_addressed: P1: eps not gated -> added eps==1e-6 gate to both predicates + 2 negative tests; re-validated 33/33
P1: H200-only not enforced in wrapper -> added capability (9,0) gate (_on_target_arch); the kda_kernels dispatcher also routes by capability
findings_noted_followups: P2: softened 'near attainable bound' to 'memory-dominated at ~75-78% peak'; absolute-bound proof (baseline NCU + measured copy roofline) is a follow-up
P2: benchmark labeled synchronized Python wall-clock (perf_counter+cudaSync); NCU kernel-only durations reported alongside
P2: geomean is equal-shape-weighted; production/time-weighted speedup is a follow-up
reason: No blockers; two P1 gate tightenings applied and re-validated; promotion re-verified on H200 (CUDA path for captured, fallback otherwise).

summary: Round-1 fixes for Round-0 review blockers: LN double-precision math (passes strict 1e-5 incl. adversarial), rank-1 dispatch gate, exact-commit benchmark, source-counter NCU.
changes: layer_norm_fp32_kernel now accumulates mean/variance + normalize in double (fp32 store); restored abs_ceiling=1e-5 for ALL fp32 incl. adversarial
gates require rank-1 weight/bias/w exact length (reject (1,N)/(N,1)) + eps==1e-6 + cap(9,0); added rank/eps negative tests
benchmark re-run with KDA_COMMIT=9c11e1cc8, exact cmd + before/after idleness in benchmark.csv
NCU: added --set source --section SourceCounters reports for rms_huge + ln; refreshed --set full
correctness: PASS 33/33 (strict 1e-5 on all fp32 perf+adversarial; rank-1 + eps negatives)

benchmark.command: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=7 PYTHONPATH=<sglang>/python:tests KDA_COMMIT=9c11e1cc8 python benchmark.py (warmup25 iters100, build warmed)

reason: All Round-0 review blockers closed. LN now correct on adversarial (1e-5) at the cost of ~26% kernel time (1.119x->1.011x, still non-regressing); RMS unchanged. Geomean 1.488x, no per-shape regression. LN throughput recovery queued.

summary: Round-2: hardened benchmark.py (fail-fast metadata + in-script before/after idleness via nvidia-smi + settle), re-benchmarked on idle H200 with full metadata, re-exported, rewrote evidence docs to consistent round-2 values.

benchmark.idle_after: util0%/mem122043MiB (a 122GB job arrived during the post-timing settle window; util0% + tight per-shape distributions confirm NO contention DURING timing)
benchmark.validity: idle_before clean (623MiB, no other resident job) + tight p10/p90 (no contention spikes) => clean timing; the shared box is heavily contended and two transient-contention attempts were detected by idle_before(util51%/mem122GB) and discarded before this clean run

docs_updated: profile/ncu_round0/REPORT.md (full round-2 rewrite, no stale round-0 text)
ncu: unchanged from s4-round1 (kernels identical): RMS huge-M DRAM 75.7%; LN double mixed 62.7%/56.7%; RMS tiny launch-bound 0.08 waves
reason: Round-1 review blockers closed: complete + verifiable benchmark metadata (commit+host+gpu+full cmd+before/after idleness), fail-fast on missing metadata so no under-documented row can be stamped, and all evidence docs consistent at round-2 values.

summary: Round-3: benchmark.py now VALIDATES + rejects busy/unavailable idleness (abort, no CSV write); captured a clean run with a genuinely idle GPU BEFORE and AFTER; re-exported from the clean row.

benchmark.idle_after: util=0% mem_used=717MiB procs=1 (own CUDA context only; no external job)
benchmark.validity: idle_before AND idle_after both validated clean (util<=5% mem<=2048MiB); benchmark.py aborts-without-writing on busy/unavailable, so only a clean before+after run can be appended/exported; tight p10/p90 confirm no contention during timing

ncu: unchanged from s4-round1 (kernels identical): RMS huge-M DRAM 75.7%; LN double mixed 62.7%/56.7%; RMS tiny launch-bound 0.08 waves
reason: FINAL ACCEPTED benchmark/export evidence: genuinely idle H200 before AND after, validated in-script (busy/unavailable runs aborted before any CSV write). Geomean 1.5126x, no per-shape regression. Stamps derive from this clean row.

summary: Round-4 code-review fixes (codex review, 2x P2): multi-GPU CUDAGuard in both kernel launchers + registry-callable routing for both wrapped entry points; re-validated + clean re-benchmark at the fix commit + re-export.
changes: src/csrc/norm_kernels.cu: c10::cuda::CUDAGuard(x.device()) in rms_norm_bf16_n128 + layer_norm_fp32 so the launch/alloc/stream target x's GPU (multi-GPU safe; single-GPU no-op)
src/register.py: optimized_wrapper routes (x,w[,eps])->triton_one_pass_rms_norm and (x,weight,bias,eps,...)->norm_infer (was always norm_infer, dropping eps for RMS)
ncu: behaviorally unchanged from s4-round1 (CUDAGuard is host-side): RMS huge-M DRAM 75.7%; LN double mixed 62.7%/56.7%; RMS tiny launch-bound 0.08 waves
reason: FINAL ACCEPTED evidence: code-review P2 correctness fixes (multi-GPU launch safety + registry routing preserving both callsite contracts) applied; clean before+after-idle benchmark at the fix commit; stamps reference 6a509a445. Fixes are benchmark-inert (numbers shift only by run-to-run variance).

summary: Round-5 code-review fix (codex review P2): optimized_wrapper now routes norm_infer(x, weight, None, eps=...) (optional None bias) to norm_infer instead of mis-routing to RMS; re-validated + clean re-benchmark at fix commit + re-export.
changes: src/register.py: 3-positional routing sends a None/Tensor 3rd arg OR an eps-kwarg to norm_infer (was Tensor-only, so None bias fell through to RMS and raised)
ncu: behaviorally unchanged (register.py-only fix): RMS huge-M DRAM 75.7%; LN double mixed 62.7%/56.7%; RMS tiny launch-bound 0.08 waves
reason: FINAL ACCEPTED: registry callable now preserves the optional-None-bias norm_infer callsite; clean before+after-idle benchmark at the fix commit; stamps reference 6aaec1397. register.py-only fix (benchmark-inert, kernels byte-identical).
